rent.note: furnished 1BR in Centro/Santa Ana; range $600–$1,100 as of 2026

utilities.note: electric + water + internet; A/C heavy in summer inflates electric bill

groceries.note: home cooking; local mercados offer very low produce prices

transit.note: combis/buses + Uber; city bikeable but hot

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base.

evidence.quote: Expats can live comfortably on $1,200–$1,500 per month, including rent

evidence.claim: 1BR in Centro $700–$1,100; exchange rate ~17.3–17.5 pesos/USD in 2026
evidence.quote: as of May 2026, the exchange rate is hovering around 17.3 to 17.5 pesos to the dollar, meaning your American income now buys about 12–15% less than it did three years ago
Studio or 1-bedroom in a quieter colonia (outside Centro) costs $400–$700/month, while Studio or 1-bedroom in Centro, Santa Ana, or Santiago runs $700–$1,100/month

evidence.claim: many expats rent 1BR in Centro for ~$600–$800
evidence.quote: Many expats rent modern one-bedroom apartments in Centro for around $600–$800 USD
